svg.title: Server Resources — Full Diagram
svg.desc: A comprehensive tree diagram of Linux server resources: CPU, Memory, Storage, and Network, with commands and explanations.
svg.text: Each core = 1 thread at a time

g.text: Swap memory — overflow space when RAM is full

svg.text: Swappiness (0–100) — controls how eagerly the kernel uses swap
Check: cat /proc/sys/vm/swappiness Set: sudo sysctl vm.swappiness=10 (lower = prefer RAM)

g.text: Process management — controlling what runs on the server
Resource limits — preventing one process from eating everything
Monitoring cheatsheet — one command for each resource

svg.text: Rule: if load avg > number of CPU cores → server is overloaded
Example: 8 cores, load avg = 8.0 → 100% busy · load avg = 16.0 → queue forming
